commit_introduced: ddcadd2 Execute Phase 24O shadow residual remediation
expected_behavior: Selected-source matching should be based on canonical source keys, document keys, binding_source_key, and span binding keys; title text should not silently become a primary selected-source key.
current_behavior: The matcher also accepts chunk metadata title fields: source_title, canonical_title, belge_adi, law_name. A title-like selected key can therefore rank/focus a chunk as selected-source matched.
safe_recovery_design: Introduce ENABLE_PHASE24W_SOURCE_IDENTITY_RECOVERY. When enabled on non-live runtimes, title metadata must not satisfy selected-source-key matching unless explicit query/source identifier evidence and compatible family/domain are present. Keep default off to preserve current live behavior.

expected_behavior: selected_source_rank should prefer chunks bound to the selected canonical document/source identity, not merely title-overlapping documents.
current_behavior: selected_source_rank consumes the broadened matcher, so title metadata can move a chunk to selected_source_rank=0 before family/title/text overlap tie-breaks.
safe_recovery_design: Gate the broadened matcher at the helper level so ranking and focusing share the same systemic behavior; do not add row-level branches.

expected_behavior: Focused chunk set should be restricted only when selected canonical source/binding keys are stable and directly match chunk identity.
current_behavior: The focus step can include title-matched chunks as selected chunks, reducing room for the originally correct source when title metadata collides with query/topic language.
safe_recovery_design: Use the same flag-gated helper; preserve canonical key and binding key matching; suppress title-only selected-source focus in recovery mode.

commit_introduced: de7c653 Phase24N data plus ddcadd2 dynamic loading path
expected_behavior: Official source supplements should repair missing body/evidence and support the evidence bundle without becoming an uncontrolled primary selection override.
current_behavior: Phase24N supplements are loaded by default when ENABLE_PHASE24N_SOURCE_SUPPLEMENTS is true and can enter source catalog/candidate surfaces.
rows_affected: KANUN-12 positive; YON-04 positive; KKY-03; possible supplement rows
safe_recovery_design: Do not revert first. Treat supplements as support-only for recovery design unless explicit query/source identity evidence and compatible family/domain exist. Preserve positive rows as guards.

expected_behavior: Article span selection should lock to canonical document/source keys and article evidence, not title metadata alone.
current_behavior: No Phase23R-E to Phase24U code change found in this file. It already checks source, document, binding, canonical source/document keys and normalized variants.
rows_affected: downstream only if source_identity hands it a wrong selected candidate
safe_recovery_design: Do not modify first. Use trace fields to confirm whether wrong source arrives before article span selection.

expected_behavior: Retrieval orchestration may enrich metadata for visibility but should not decide primary source identity.
current_behavior: No audited code change. It enriches metadata with source title before downstream selection; risk appears only when downstream selected-source matching treats titles as identity keys.
rows_affected: KANUN-08; YON-05 only through source_identity consumption of title metadata
safe_recovery_design: Leave unchanged. Constrain title usage in source_identity instead of removing metadata enrichment.

expected_behavior: When selected source/span are unchanged, claimed family/identifier and failure classes should not degrade without a trace-visible contract reason.
current_behavior: KANUN-02, MULGA-04, and YON-08 regressed with the same selected source/span, so source selection alone is insufficient.
safe_recovery_design: Do not mix with source_identity recovery. Use Phase24W-B trace/failure audit to determine answer_contract_surface vs answer_slot_completeness vs scorer_proxy_only.
